decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly getting Arclight Phoenix onto the battlefield by using spells with low mana cost like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse. Treasure Cruise and Consider help to fill up the graveyard with spells to bring back the Phoenix. Picklock Prankster can also help to trigger the Phoenix's ability. Opt, Sleight of Hand, and Spirebluff Canal ensure consistency in drawing into the necessary cards. The deck also includes some removal spells like Spikefield Hazard and Pyroclasm to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to be fast and aggressive in order to overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, which allows the player to create a powerful flying token army. The key strategy involves using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board while ramping up to play Parhelion II. By combining Greasefang, Okiba Boss with Guardian of New Benalia and Fleeting Spirit, the player can create a loop of sacrificing creatures to generate flying tokens and overwhelm the opponent. Monument to Endurance and Bitter Triumph help protect and support the combo, providing resilience against removal and other disruptions.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy with a combination of hand disruption like Thoughtseize and Duress, removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and powerful threats like Liliana of the Veil and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. It also utilizes mana acceleration with Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to quickly ramp into its higher cost spells. The inclusion of cards like Unholy Annex/Ritual Chamber and Darkbore Pathway provide additional versatility and consistency. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through disruption and removal while establishing a strong board presence with its threats.

The deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent quickly by playing low-cost aggressive creatures and spells to deal damage fast. It uses cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger to chip away at the opponent's life total while using burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to clear the way. The deck also includes some card draw and mana fixing with cards like Academic Dispute and Spirebluff Canal. Overall, the strategy is to maintain a fast pace and overwhelm the opponent before they can set up their defenses.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Greasefang Parhelion combo to overwhelm opponents with strong creatures and powerful abilities. The deck includes ways to protect and support the combo pieces, with cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The main strategy is to set up the combo as quickly as possible while controlling the game with removal and disruption spells. The deck aims to quickly overpower the opponent and secure victory through the synergy of its key cards.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. Questing Druid and Kumano Faces Kakkazan provide additional versatility and threats to the board. The inclusion of cards like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins adds extra reach in the late game. Overall, the deck's strategy is to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize.

This Red Deck Wins Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures with cheap mana costs and using burn spells to clear the way for an aggressive offense. Sunspine Lynx and Monastery Swiftspear provide early pressure, while Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Emberheart Challenger offer powerful threats. Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage can help deal additional damage, while cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Lightning Strike provide removal and direct damage. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and efficient game plan, taking advantage of its low curve and high damage output.

The deck focuses on using control elements such as counterspells, removal, and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan while keeping the board clear. It utilizes a mix of sweepers like Supreme Verdict and spot removal like Dovin's Veto to control the battlefield. The deck also includes card draw and card selection to ensure it has the right answers at the right time. Additionally, the inclusion of the companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides a powerful, synergistic option that can add value to the deck's game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful legendary creatures to create a fast-paced and aggressive strategy. By combining various types of legends that work well together, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly before they have a chance to establish a strong defense. The inclusion of cards like Arachne, Rona, and Jirina provides both board presence and synergy with other legendary creatures, while support cards like Relic of Legends and Plaza of Heroes help to boost the effectiveness of the legends in play. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a strong offense and pressure the opponent from the early turns of the game.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures and utilizing burn spells to control the board and deal damage to the opponent. Academic Dispute and Heroes' Hangout provide card advantage and tempo swings, while cards like Emberheart Challenger and Soul-Scar Mage put pressure on the opponent early on. The deck also includes some disruptive elements like Boomerang Basics and Reckless Rage to deal with opposing threats. The mana base is optimized to allow for consistent aggressive plays with a mix of Mountains and dual lands.

The primary focus of this deck is to consistently apply pressure on the opponent through a combination of low-cost aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. The deck aims to quickly establish a board presence with cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage while also using spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to remove threats and deal damage directly to the opponent. Additionally, cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Cori-Steel Cutter help to increase the deck's overall tempo and provide additional ways to close out the game quickly.

This control deck revolves around the powerful card advantage and selection provided by Niv-Mizzet Reborn. The deck is designed to ramp into Niv-Mizzet Reborn as quickly as possible using mana dorks like Sylvan Caryatid and then utilize cards like Bring to Light to find answers to the opponent's threats. With powerful companions like Yorion, Sky Nomad enhancing the deck's capabilities and a diverse mana base to support the various multicolored cards in the deck, this archetype aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a grindy game.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures such as Aven Interrupter, Archon of Emeria, and Voice of Resurgence to overwhelm opponents. It also leverages efficient mana acceleration with cards like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to ramp into powerful threats like Skyclave Apparition and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Settle the Wreckage and Collected Company to control the board and maintain pressure. With a mix of early aggression and disruptive elements, this deck aims to put opponents on the back foot and secure a fast victory.

The main idea of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent with quick and efficient creatures such as Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger. Utilizing spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the opponent's board and deal direct damage. The deck also includes card draw and selection with Consider and Academic Dispute, as well as some tempo plays with cards like Brazen Borrower and Boomerang Basics. The overall strategy focuses on dealing damage quickly and efficiently, while also maintaining card advantage and board control.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By using cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to slow down the opponent, while developing a board presence with cards like Skyclave Apparition and Collected Company. The goal is to establish early pressure and maintain momentum throughout the game, utilizing the synergy between creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to accelerate your game plan. The deck aims to close out the game quickly through a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells.

The main idea of this control deck is to utilize Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful card advantage engine, with the ability to draw multiple cards if properly built around. The deck includes a variety of different colors to enable the casting of Niv-Mizzet, along with a toolbox of versatile spells like Bring to Light and Supreme Verdict to control the game. The companion Yorion provides additional value and flexibility in longer games. Overall, the deck aims to control the board and outvalue opponents with its versatile cards and powerful creatures.
